mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals robust underlying economic momentum, which translates into higher expected corporate earnings and drives positive equity returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-GFC labor market recovery is ongoing
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward rank information coefficient (IC) is consistently negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero over any rolling 36-month period.

mechanism_narrative: While the federal funds rate is pinned at the zero lower bound, an upside surprise in monthly inflation (CPIAUCSL) will increase market expectations of a premature monetary tightening cycle, raising the discount rate and causing negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in over 50% of instances where the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of accelerating real retail sales (RSAFS) and a falling unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a self-reinforcing cycle of strong consumer demand and income growth, which is a powerful tailwind for corporate revenues and equity prices.
failure_condition: The combined signal (e.g., an equal-weighted z-score) fails to show a rank IC greater than 0.05 over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery, strong growth in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates that supply chains are normalizing and corporate operating leverage is high, leading to better-than-expected earnings and positive short-term equity performance.
evidence_refs: fact:Economy is 5+ years into recovery from 2008 crisis
failure_condition: The correlation between 3-month changes in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative during the 2010-2015 period.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below a key threshold, it signals a tight labor market that will lead to wage pressures, compressing corporate profit margins and causing a negative repricing of equities over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE has fallen from 10% to 5.6% since 2009
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE is below 5.8%, average 60-day forward returns are not statistically lower than periods where it is above 5.8% (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A persistently low and stable federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) acts as a direct support for equity valuations by keeping the risk-free discount rate low, boosting the present value of future earnings and supporting positive short-term returns.
failure_condition: The average 5-day forward return when FEDFUNDS is in its lowest quartile is not significantly different from the average return when it is in its highest quartile.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent slowdown in both housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a broad-based deterioration in consumer confidence and spending, which precedes negative earnings revisions and leads to negative equity market performance.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances following the trigger condition being met.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-interest-rate environment, an unexpectedly rapid decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is a leading indicator for the central bank reaching its mandate, forcing a hawkish policy shift that negatively impacts equity markets.
evidence_refs: fact:Market is pricing the first rate hike post-GFC
failure_condition: The signal's hit rate (predicting negative returns) is below 50% during the 2013-2015 period when the 'taper tantrum' and rate hike speculation were prominent.
